what_it_does: A conversation-capture 'ideas round' note (2026-06-11) enumerating fifteen unconventional uses of an embedding layer, grouped into six kinds, to resolve the inconsistency and intent-loss problems of a context-less, spec-less codebase. It opens with a framing inversion (Tim's utterances are truth, code is the decaying echo) and ends by naming the three highest-value sweeps.
observations: Opens with Tim's verbatim ask for 'heaps of ideas, different kinds.'

States a load-bearing framing inversion: conventionally code is truth and docs decay; HERE Tim's utterances are truth and code is the decaying echo, so the primary embedding axis is utterance<->artifact, not doc<->code.

Organizes 15 numbered ideas under six 'Kinds': dissolving inconsistency, reuniting intent with implementation, cold sessions stop being cold, verification without specifications, speaking at Tim's altitude, structure that grows itself.

Kind 2 is flagged 'the deepest vein' and includes #4 THE PROMISE LEDGER (Tim-utterance-of-wanting with no implementation match = undelivered promise), called 'likely the single highest-value sweep available.'
Several ideas reference existing system patterns parenthetically (built-twice radar, M3 corroboration, incomplete-work law, ai_tics inversion, the wire's scope-derivation).
#12 semantic addressing example: 'the breathing circle thing' -> ui://cognition/pulse, pointing by description not canonical names.

Closing 'thread' names the embedding layer as connective tissue replacing a long-tenured engineer's familiarity, and identifies a STRUCTURAL THREE: #4 promise ledger, #7 boot-by-similarity, #9 collision detection.
standouts: The framing inversion that utterances are truth and code is the decaying echo, making utterance<->artifact the primary embedding axis.
The PROMISE LEDGER (#4) — embedding wants against implementations to emit the undelivered-promise list.
The STRUCTURAL THREE called out as the head-on attack on 'no one knows everything built.'
conventions: Follows the conversation-capture note style: Tim's ask quoted up front, a bold load-bearing framing, numbered ideas with parenthetical links to prior system concepts.
Uses the vault habit of naming a small high-priority subset at the end rather than ranking the whole list.
Very terse — a brainstorm capture, not an expanded spec; companion to TOTAL-PROCESSING-PIPELINES.md.
notes: A compact brainstorm of 15 embedding-powered sweeps (across six kinds) for a spec-less, inconsistent estate, anchored by the utterances-are-truth inversion and the promise ledger as the top sweep.
questions: Which of these 15 ideas were carried into the TOTAL-PROCESSING-PIPELINES spec versus left as raw ideas?
Has the promise ledger (#4) actually been built or run?
What threshold/method governs the auto-proposed relations (#14)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it is an explicit ideas round and delivers a wide spread of distinct embedding-use ideas without committing to implementation, exactly as the ask requested.
apparent_themes: Reuniting intent with implementation via meaning-search
Embeddings as connective tissue replacing institutional familiarity
intention_signals: Trying to generate the full breadth of ways an embedding layer can compensate for the absence of specs, plans, shared context, and human review — surfacing candidates so the highest-value ones (the structural three) can be prioritized.
novelty: The promise ledger and idea-genealogy framings, and the inversion making Tim's utterances the embedding ground truth rather than code or docs.
contribution: Contributes a catalogued idea-space of fifteen embedding uses across six kinds, the utterances-are-truth framing, and the named structural-three priority set (promise ledger, boot-by-similarity, collision detection).
summary_for_embedding: A brainstorm note listing fifteen unconventional embedding uses across six kinds (concept unification, promise ledger, intent triangulation, boot-by-similarity, done-ness-as-proximity, semantic addressing, auto-proposed relations, idea genealogies) to resolve a spec-less inconsistent estate, framed on utterances-as-truth.
intention_for_embedding: To enumerate, without ranking the whole set, the many ways an embedding layer can replace missing specs and institutional memory and to flag the three highest-value sweeps.
